data.title: Jump Trading calls Terraform Labs' $4.4 billion lawsuit a "scapegoating"
data.description: Jump Trading responded on Monday to a lawsuit filed by Todd Snyder, head of the Terraform Labs bankruptcy trusteeship. Snyder sued Jump Trading, several of its subsidiaries, and two executives last December, alleging market manipulation, investor fraud, and self-dealing, seeking $4 billion in damages. In its response, Jump Trading called the lawsuit a “transparent attempt” by Snyder to evade the $4.4 billion fine imposed on Terraform Labs by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in 2024, and stated that Snyder “fabricated a series of allegations aimed at shifting Terraform’s liability to the SEC and its creditors onto the defendants.” Jump Trading also pointed out that the lawsuit lacked crucial details, failed to specify the actions of each defendant, did not identify the locations of the alleged violations, and was time-barred and should be dismissed. According to previous court records, when the UST de-pegged to the dollar in 2021, Jump Trading assisted Terraform Labs in maintaining the UST's peg by buying large amounts of UST to support its price, but executives from both sides kept this confidential. Terraform Labs founder Do Kwon had previously been sentenced to 15 years in prison for two counts of fraud.

data.title: Seven newly created wallets withdrew nearly 75,000 ETH from Binance within 16 hours, worth approximately $161 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past 16 hours, seven newly created wallets withdrew a total of 74,959 ETH from Binance, worth approximately $161 million, and then transferred the ETH to other wallets.

data.title: Merck's All-Cash Deal to Value Terns at $6 Billion, Financial Times Reports
data.description: Merck is reportedly set to acquire Terns in an all-cash transaction, valuing the company at approximately $6 billion, according to the Financial Times. This acquisition highlights Merck's strategic move to expand its portfolio and strengthen its position in the pharmaceutical industry. The deal is expected to enhance Merck's capabilities and market reach, providing significant growth opportunities. Further details about the transaction and its implications for both companies are anticipated as the deal progresses.

data.title: Merck Nears Final Stage in Talks with Terns, Agreement Expected Soon
data.description: Merck is reportedly in the late stages of negotiations with Terns, with a potential agreement anticipated in the coming days. According to Jin10, the discussions have progressed significantly, indicating a possible conclusion shortly. The details of the agreement remain undisclosed, but the advancement in talks suggests a strategic collaboration between the two companies. This development highlights Merck's ongoing efforts to expand its partnerships and enhance its market presence.

data.title: Hong Kong ADRs Decline Amid U.S. Stock Market Downturn
data.description: Hong Kong ADRs fell by 120 points or 0.48%, closing at 24,944 points as the U.S. stock market experienced a downturn. According to Ming Pao, the immediate month Hang Seng Index futures rose by 11 points, reaching 24,972 points, with the futures trading at a discount of 92 points.

data.title: BlackRock CEO: Crypto business could bring in $500 million in annual revenue for the company over the next five years.
data.description: In his 2026 annual letter to shareholders, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink stated that the crypto business and other high-growth markets could generate $500 million in revenue for the company over the next five years. BlackRock currently manages nearly 800,000 BTC, worth approximately $55 billion, through its spot Bitcoin ETF, and its iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF is estimated to generate approximately $250 million in annual fees. Furthermore, BlackRock's USD institutional digital liquidity fund, Buidl, has become the world's largest tokenized fund, with assets under management exceeding $2 billion. Fink stated in the letter that BlackRock manages nearly $150 billion in related assets in the digital asset space, including approximately $65 billion in stablecoin reserves and nearly $80 billion in digital asset exchange-traded products. He stated that tokenization could "update the infrastructure of the financial system," comparing the development of blockchain tokenization to the rapid development of the internet in the 1990s. Fink also pointed out that if the United States does not invest enough in digitalization and tokenization, other countries will surpass the US.

data.title: Australian Inflation Remains High Amid Global Tensions
data.description: Australian inflation continued to be elevated in February, Bloomberg posted on X, despite the onset of the Iran war which disrupted energy supplies in the Middle East and led to a surge in gasoline prices. This situation underscores ongoing price pressures within the Australian economy.

data.title: Indonesia's Markets Face Volatility Post-Holiday
data.description: Indonesia's financial markets are expected to experience volatility as they reopen following a week-long holiday. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that investors may face fluctuating sentiment due to various economic factors. The reopening comes amid global economic uncertainties, which could influence market dynamics in the region. Analysts suggest that traders should be prepared for potential shifts in market trends as trading resumes.

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 2,267 BTC from Coinbase in the past 10 hours, worth approximately $158 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock withdrew 2,267 BTC from Coinbase in the past 10 hours, worth approximately $158 million.

data.title: Japan's 10-Year Government Bond Yield Falls by 2.0 Basis Points
data.description: The yield on Japan's 10-year government bonds decreased by 2.0 basis points, settling at 2.245%. According to Jin10, this decline reflects ongoing fluctuations in the bond market. The movement in yields is closely watched by investors as it can influence borrowing costs and economic conditions. The change in yield comes amid broader market dynamics and economic indicators that continue to shape investor sentiment.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank Anticipates Economic Slack Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: New Zealand's central bank has identified persistent slack in the economy, which will influence its approach to addressing inflationary pressures resulting from increased oil prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's cautious stance as it navigates the economic landscape. The central bank's assessment comes amid global concerns over rising energy costs and their impact on inflation rates. As the country grapples with these challenges, the bank's strategy will be crucial in maintaining economic stability. The central bank's observations underscore the complexity of balancing inflation control with economic growth, as policymakers weigh their options in response to fluctuating oil prices.

data.title: Asian Currencies Steady Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Asian currencies remained stable as traders closely monitored developments in the Middle East. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the geopolitical situation in the region has led to cautious trading, with investors assessing potential impacts on global markets. The stability in Asian currencies comes despite ongoing uncertainties, as market participants remain vigilant about any significant changes that could affect currency valuations. Analysts suggest that while the immediate impact on Asian currencies has been limited, prolonged tensions could lead to increased volatility. Traders are advised to stay informed about the evolving situation and its possible implications for the financial markets.

data.title: ARK Invest Acquires Circle Internet Group Shares, Sells Other Holdings
data.description:
ARK Invest recently purchased 161,513 shares of Circle Internet Group (NASDAQ: CRCL), valued at approximately $20.45 million. According to ChainCatcher, the stock closed down 20.11% on the same day. 

In terms of sales, ARK reduced its holdings in Twist Bioscience Corp (NASDAQ: TWST) by 190,100 shares, worth around $8.46 million, marking its largest sale of the day. 

Additionally, ARK sold 54,640 shares of Roku Inc (NASDAQ: ROKU) for about $5.34 million, 18,620 shares of Teradyne Inc (NASDAQ: TER) for approximately $5.66 million, and a total of 41,064 shares of Bullish (BLSH) across its ARKK, ARKW, and ARKF ETFs, valued at around $1.62 million.

data.title: Crude oil futures prices plunge
data.description: Near-month WTI crude oil futures fell 5.9% to $86.86 per barrel. Near-month Brent crude oil futures fell 6.6% to $97.57 per barrel. (Jinshi)

data.title: Two new wallets, suspected to be linked to Bitmine, withdrew 67,100 ETH from Kraken.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, two newly created wallets withdrew 67,111 ETH from Kraken, worth approximately $145 million, which is suspected to be related to Bitmine.

data.title: Global Efforts Intensify to Secure Crop Nutrients Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Governments worldwide are urgently working to secure essential crop nutrients as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East disrupts commodity flows, raising concerns about a potential global food crisis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increased efforts by nations to ensure adequate supplies for the upcoming spring planting season. The disruption in the supply chain has intensified fears of shortages, prompting swift action from various governments to mitigate the impact on agricultural production. The situation underscores the critical importance of securing these vital resources to maintain food security globally.

data.title: Platform X will update its revenue sharing mechanism, increasing the weighting of creators' regional exposure.
data.description: Nikita Bier, Product Manager at X, announced on the X platform that starting Thursday, the platform will update its revenue-sharing incentive mechanism, increasing the weight of creators' regional exposure to encourage content that resonates with users in their home country, neighboring countries, and those speaking the same language. This move aims to reduce attention-grabbing tactics and promote diverse dialogue on the platform. X invites creators to begin building their local audiences.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise Amid Mixed Developments; Trump Hints at Iran Negotiations
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields have increased, with yield spreads showing varied developments, leading to declines in the Golden Dragon Index and U.S. stocks. According to Ming Pao, U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested that Iran has presented a gift related to the Strait of Hormuz, with partial agreement reached on potential deal terms during negotiations. Reports indicate that the United States has submitted a 15-point plan to Iran aimed at ending conflicts, with claims that Washington seeks a one-month ceasefire to advance talks. Meanwhile, Gulf Arab states are reportedly considering joining U.S. and Israeli military actions against Iran. The Trump administration has ordered the deployment of approximately 2,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East.

data.title: SK Hynix Plans U.S. ADR Offering Amid Expansion Goals
data.description:
South Korean chip manufacturer SK Hynix has announced its intention to issue American Depositary Receipts (ADR) by submitting confidential filing materials to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). According to RTHK, the company has also filed documents with South Korean regulatory authorities, indicating plans to complete the listing within this year. However, details regarding the issuance size, structure, and timeline have yet to be finalized.

Previous reports from South Korean media suggested that SK Hynix is considering raising between 10 trillion to 15 trillion Korean won, approximately $6.7 billion to $10 billion, through the U.S. listing. The company aims to expand its production capacity for advanced memory chips as part of its growth strategy.

data.title: Meta Faces Challenges in EU Rollout of Ray-Ban Smart Glasses
data.description: Meta's introduction of its new Ray-Ban smart glasses in the European Union is encountering obstacles due to battery and artificial intelligence regulations, as well as supply limitations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting these challenges as Meta attempts to expand its presence in the wearable technology market. The regulatory environment in the EU is proving to be a significant hurdle for the tech giant, impacting the availability and distribution of the smart glasses. Despite these setbacks, Meta remains committed to addressing these issues to ensure a successful launch in the region.
